title: Webhooks: Receive & Send Data
lede: Webhooks turn iMash into the hub of your whole stack. Point any form, app, or script at a unique URL and it can start an automation the instant something happens — and after every call, iMash can push summaries, transcripts, and extracted data back to your own systems. This guide covers both directions, A to Z.

steps.title: Open the Automation Flows Dashboard
steps.content:
Incoming webhooks live inside the flow builder, so that's where we start.

  1. In the left sidebar, under Intelligence Center, click Automation (or use the top tabs: Automation → Automation Flows).
  2. The Flows dashboard opens. Here you can see every flow, plus two more tabs:
    • Flows — your list of automations, with name, steps, folder, last-modified date, and status.
    • Runs — the execution history of every flow, including each webhook delivery.
    • Issues — failed runs that need attention.
  3. On the left of the dashboard you'll also find Folders (organize flows), Custom Tables, System Tables, MCP, Todos, and Connections.

images.alt: Automation Flows dashboard with the Flows list and Create flow button
images.caption: The Flows dashboard — every webhook-triggered automation starts here.

images.alt: Automation Flows dashboard with the Flows list and Create flow button
images.caption: The Flows dashboard — every webhook-triggered automation starts here.

steps.title: Create a New Flow
steps.content:
1. Click Create flow in the top-right of the dashboard.
  2. Choose From scratch.
  3. The builder opens with the flow name selected — type a descriptive name (for example Webhook Lead Intake) and press Enter.

You now have an empty canvas with a single step: 1. Select Trigger. The trigger is what starts the flow — and for webhooks, that trigger is an incoming HTTP request.

steps.title: Choose the Catch Webhook Trigger
steps.content:
1. Click the 1. Select Trigger step on the canvas. The trigger picker opens.
  2. Type webhook in the Search box. You can also browse by category — All, AI, Core, or Apps.
  3. Under the Webhook piece, click Catch Webhook — it "receives HTTP requests and triggers flows using unique URLs."
💡 Tip: The search also surfaces app-specific webhook triggers (Drupal, Fountain, and more). For a generic "any system can call this" endpoint, Catch Webhook is the one you want.

images.alt: Trigger picker with 'webhook' typed in the search box and Catch Webhook listed
images.caption: Search "webhook" and pick Catch Webhook under the Webhook piece.

images.alt: Trigger picker with 'webhook' typed in the search box and Catch Webhook listed
images.caption: Search "webhook" and pick Catch Webhook under the Webhook piece.

steps.title: Copy Your Unique Webhook URL
steps.content:
The moment you select Catch Webhook, iMash generates a URL that belongs to this flow alone. The configuration panel on the right shows everything you need:

  • Live URL — the production endpoint. Click the copy icon next to it. Requests sent here trigger the flow once it's published.
  • Test URL — add /test to the end of the Live URL. Requests sent here are captured as sample data while you build, without publishing anything.
  • Synchronous Requests — if the calling system expects a response back, add /sync to the end of the URL. The flow then has up to 30 seconds to answer (otherwise the caller receives a 408 Request Timeout). To return data, add a Webhook step with the Return Response action at the end of your flow.
  1. Copy the Live URL.
  2. Paste it into the outside system that should trigger this flow — a website form, Zapier, your CRM, or your own code. Any HTTP method with JSON, form data, or query parameters works.
⚠️ Heads-up: The Live URL only fires after you Publish the flow. While building, use the /test URL to capture sample requests.

images.alt: Catch Webhook configuration panel showing the Live URL, Synchronous Requests note, and Test Trigger button
images.caption: The Catch Webhook panel: Live URL, /sync behavior, and Generate Sample Data.

images.alt: Catch Webhook configuration panel showing the Live URL, Synchronous Requests note, and Test Trigger button
images.caption: The Catch Webhook panel: Live URL, /sync behavior, and Generate Sample Data.

steps.title: Send a Test Request & Capture Sample Data
steps.content:
Before you can map incoming fields into actions, iMash needs to see one real example of the data.

  1. In the Generate Sample Data section at the bottom of the panel, click Test Trigger.
  2. From the outside system (or a quick curl), send a request to the Test URL (the Live URL + /test). For example:
    curl -X POST <your-test-url> -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"first_name":"Dana","phone":"+1 555 010 7788"}'
  3. The captured request appears as Result #1, broken down into headers, body, queryParams, and rawBody.

Every field you see here — body.first_name, body.phone, and so on — is now available to every later step through the Data Selector.

images.alt: Captured webhook sample showing the parsed body fields of the test request
images.caption: The captured request: every body field becomes mappable data for your flow steps.

images.alt: Captured webhook sample showing the parsed body fields of the test request
images.caption: The captured request: every body field becomes mappable data for your flow steps.

steps.title: Add Actions & Publish
steps.content:
Now make the webhook do something.

  1. Click the + button under the trigger step to add an action — for example a System Tables action to create a lead, an outbound call action to have a Smart Agent dial the new contact, or an SMS step.
  2. In each action's fields, use the Data Selector to map the webhook's fields (like body.first_name) into the action.
  3. Use Test Flow at the top of the canvas to run the whole flow against your sample data.
  4. When it works, click Publish in the top-right. From this moment the Live URL is armed — every request starts a run.
💡 Tip: Check the Runs tab on the Flows dashboard to see each incoming request and exactly what every step did with it — perfect for debugging a misbehaving integration.

images.alt: Flow builder canvas with the Catch Webhook trigger configured and the Publish button visible
images.caption: Catch Webhook in place — add actions below it, test, then Publish.

images.alt: Flow builder canvas with the Catch Webhook trigger configured and the Publish button visible
images.caption: Catch Webhook in place — add actions below it, test, then Publish.

steps.title: Send Data OUT: Post-Call Webhooks
steps.content:
Webhooks flow the other way too — after every call, your Smart Agent can POST rich results to any URL you own.

  1. Go to Intelligence Center → Smart Agents and open your agent.
  2. Scroll to the Webhooks Post Processing row and click it.
  3. In Server URL & Events, paste the endpoint that should receive the data, then tick the Webhook Events you want delivered:
    • Summary — an AI-written recap of the call.
    • Success Evaluation — whether the call met its goal, scored by your chosen rubric.
    • Structured Data — custom JSON fields extracted from the transcript using your own schema.
    • Transcript — the full conversation text.
    • Status Updates — real-time events like call started and call ended.
    • Tool Calls — details of every tool the agent used mid-conversation.

For the full walkthrough of prompts, rubrics, and the data schema builder, see Post-Call Processing & Webhooks in the Smart Agents section.

images.alt: Webhooks Post Processing modal on a Smart Agent with Server URL & Events selected
images.caption: The agent-side webhook hub: Server URL & Events, Summary, Success Evaluation, Structured Data.

images.alt: Webhooks Post Processing modal on a Smart Agent with Server URL & Events selected
images.caption: The agent-side webhook hub: Server URL & Events, Summary, Success Evaluation, Structured Data.

images.alt: Server URL field and the list of webhook event checkboxes
images.caption: Pick exactly which events get POSTed to your Server URL.

images.alt: Server URL field and the list of webhook event checkboxes
images.caption: Pick exactly which events get POSTed to your Server URL.

steps.title: Bonus: Send Data MID-Call with Custom Tools
steps.content:
Post-call webhooks fire after the conversation ends. If you need data pushed while the caller is still on the line — booking a slot, checking an order, notifying your team — give the agent a Custom Tool:

  1. Create a Custom Tool with a POST endpoint pointing at your system.
  2. Define the parameters the AI should fill from the conversation (name, callback time, issue description…).
  3. Attach the tool to your agent — it will call your URL live, mid-conversation, whenever the moment is right.

And here's the beautiful part: point that Custom Tool at a Catch Webhook URL from step 4, and your agents and automations talk to each other — a closed loop with zero code.

support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io

metaTitle: Webhooks: Receive & Send Data | iMash Documentation
metaDescription: Receive data into iMash with the Catch Webhook trigger — unique URLs, test requests, sample data — and send call results out with post-call webhooks and mid-call Custom Tools.
